_meta.purpose: Pinned offline regression baseline for class-term / brand-list completeness.
_meta.complementsExisting: test-drug-brand-coverage.js guards rules/drug-rules.json drug.match via real drugMatchesRule(); this file guards the hand-maintained JS term tables in engine/stopp-start.js, engine/acb-scores.js, and content-scripts/triage-lens/content.js — the other hand that has no guard and has already drifted.
_meta.updatePolicy: Add rows here when you extend a term list in product code (Wave C canonicalisation). Never remove rows — they are regression sentinels. Each class has a source comment field documenting provenance.

stopp_nsaid._source: UK BNF 10.1.1 / MHRA NSAID class / PINCER rules (alert-library.json). The alert-library has the most complete list (22 generics); stopp-start.js covers only 6 generics + 5 brands — the remainder are genuine gaps that Wave C will fill.
stopp_nsaid._matchMechanism: computeStoppStart() hasDrug() — case-insensitive substring of drug.label

mustMatchAll.note: UK ibuprofen brand — present in NSAID_TERMS
UK diclofenac brand — present in NSAID_TERMS
UK etoricoxib brand — present in NSAID_TERMS
UK meloxicam brand — present in NSAID_TERMS

knownGaps.clinicalRisk: A patient on piroxicam+eGFR<50 silently misses STOPP NSAID_CKD flag

knownGaps.note: This is an aspirin brand — listed here to flag confusion; it is NOT an NSAID

stopp_aspirin_lowdose._source: BNF 2.9 (antiplatelet drugs). UK low-dose aspirin forms: generic dm+d names, Nu-seals (AstraZeneca), Caprin (Pinewood), Micropirin (M&A Pharmachem). The aspirin STOPP check (stopp_aspirin_primary_prev) requires matching low-dose antiplatelet forms without catching analgesic doses.
stopp_aspirin_lowdose._matchMechanism: computeStoppStart() hasDrug() — case-insensitive substring of drug.label against ASPIRIN_TERMS

knownGaps.presentIn: nowhere in the suite — brand not covered by any aspirin term list except content.js ANTIPLATELET broad /aspirin/i regex
knownGaps.clinicalRisk: A patient whose repeat prescription shows 'Nu-seals 75mg tablets' silently misses the STOPP primary-prevention aspirin check. Nu-seals is the most common UK branded aspirin 75mg preparation (AstraZeneca; NHS prescriptions persist).
knownGaps.status: KNOWN-GAP — documented here so Wave C term canonicalisation fixes it; this test assertion is a soft-check that PRINTS the gap but does not cause exit(1)

knownGaps.clinicalRisk: Silent miss on Caprin (Pinewood Healthcare UK aspirin 75mg brand)

knownGaps.note: Word-order variant of the dm+d name — 'gastro-resistant' comes before '75mg'. Does NOT contain the substring 'aspirin 75' so hasDrug() returns false.
knownGaps.clinicalRisk: Medicus may render the dm+d name with word order 'aspirin gastro-resistant Xmg' — such a patient silently misses the STOPP check.

acb_scores_score3._source: Boustani ACB scale (ACBcalc.com). Score 3 = strong/definite anticholinergic. Source file: engine/acb-scores.js ACB_TABLE entries with score:3.

acb_scores_score2._source: Boustani ACB scale. Score 2 = moderate anticholinergic. Smaller set on the Boustani list.

content_js_anticholinergic._source: content-scripts/triage-lens/content.js ANTICHOLINERGIC table (~line 1176). This is the SEPARATE, HARDCODED table used for in-queue ACB scoring — it does NOT import engine/acb-scores.js. Divergences from acb-scores.js are documented in knownDivergences below.
content_js_anticholinergic._matchMechanism: content.js evaluates each drug against ANTICHOLINERGIC[].match (RegExp) and sums .score
content_js_anticholinergic._divergenceWarning: This table and engine/acb-scores.js cover the SAME clinical concept (ACB burden) but are maintained independently and have already drifted. The cross-file agreement assertion in test-term-coverage.js documents every divergence.

mustMatchAll.note: TCA (dothiepin) — in content.js ONLY; missing from acb-scores.js
bladder AM — present in both files
bladder AM — present in both files
bladder AM — present in both files
bladder AM — present in both files

knownDivergences.acbScoresJs: MISSING — dosulepin not in acb-scores.js ACB_TABLE at all
knownDivergences.clinicalRisk: A patient on dosulepin scores ACB 3 in the queue HUD but 0 in the engine/record view

knownDivergences.note: content.js scores trospium as 3 (same group as oxybutynin); acb-scores.js assigns 1 (quaternary — limited CNS penetration). Clinically the acb-scores.js rationale is defensible but inconsistent with content.js.
Over-scoring in queue HUD; loperamide peripheral action, limited CNS penetration
In content.js only; amantadine has anticholinergic properties
In content.js only; H2-blocker with some CNS ACB effect
In content.js only; weak evidence for anticholinergic activity
In content.js only; metformin has no recognised anticholinergic activity — likely a false positive in content.js

knownDivergences.clinicalRisk: clomipramine not detected by content.js ACB HUD — patient on clomipramine scores 0 in queue
doxepin not detected by content.js — silent miss
diphenhydramine (in OTC sleep aids, allergy products) not detected by content.js
olanzapine not in content.js ANTICHOLINERGIC table — patient on olanzapine scores 0 in queue HUD
quetiapine not in content.js — silent miss in queue ACB chip

knownDivergences.note: clozapine IS in content.js HIGH_RISK_DRUGS (monitoring) but NOT in ANTICHOLINERGIC (scoring)

knownDivergences.clinicalRisk: Silent miss in queue ACB HUD
